summary: The paper's mathematical framework is sound. The quantum experiential density definition, superoperator construction, RK4 integrator, and falsification logic are all correct. The key mechanism -- that the exchange Hamiltonian preserves |00> and |11> populations while dephasing destroys coherences, forcing the ratio I/S_B to decrease monotonically from 2 to 1 without entering the under-correlated regime -- is analytically provable. One minor numerical error was found: a factor-of-two mistake in a quoted rho_Q value on line 402 (-0.693 should be -1.386 nats). This does not affect any conclusion. All code logic was verified against the stated equations.
strengths: Equations are standard quantum information theory applied to a well-defined functional; no novel mathematical machinery that could hide errors
Superoperator construction matches the standard vectorization identity for column-stacking and is verified against explicit matrix computation to machine precision
The r >= 1 mechanism is analytically provable: H|00> = H|11> = 0 ensures populations are time-invariant, so S_BM increases monotonically from 0 to S_B, keeping r in [1,2]
Numerical validation is thorough: 10 quantum info checks, 6 Lindblad checks, RK4 fourth-order convergence confirmed, trace preserved to 1e-16
The falsification logic is clean: mu_Q = 0 identically follows necessarily from rho_Q <= 0 everywhere, which follows from r >= 1 throughout
Code logic faithfully implements the stated equations with no ad hoc corrections or hard-coded values

findings.summary: Factor-of-two error in quoted rho_Q value for pilot trajectory
findings.rationale: Line 402 states rho_Q = -0.693 nats for the theta=pi/4 pilot trajectory. Given I_vN = 2*ln(2) = 1.386 and S_B = ln(2) = 0.693, the correct value is rho_Q = I*(1-I/S_B) = 1.386*(1-2) = -1.386 nats, not -0.693 nats. The code computes the correct value; only the narrative text is wrong.
findings.required_action: Correct the quoted value from -0.693 to -1.386 nats on line 402

equations_checked.check: Direct quantum analogue of classical Eq. (1); code implements exactly this formula
Schmidt decomposition gives S_BM=0, S_B=S_M, hence I=2*S_B. Substitution gives rho_Q = 2*S_B*(1-2) = -2*S_B

equations_checked.equation: L_super = -i(I kron H - H^T kron I) + dissipator terms
equations_checked.check: Verified against column-stacking identity vec(A rho B) = (B^T kron A) vec(rho). Each term matches. Code matches. Oracle verification to 0.0 error.
Derived from L = sqrt(gamma_D)*sigma_z kron I: L^dag L = gamma_D*I, dissipator rate = -2*gamma_D on (0,3) element. Verified numerically to 5e-11 relative error.

equations_checked.equation: H = g*(sigma_x kron sigma_x + sigma_y kron sigma_y) has H|00> = H|11> = 0
equations_checked.check: Sum of Kronecker products gives 2g*(|01><10| + |10><01|), which annihilates |00> and |11>. This ensures populations in the initial-state subspace are time-invariant under H.

limits_and_crosschecks.limit: Late-time diagonal state matches static calculation

unchecked_risk_areas.reason: Only 3 spot-check trajectories; full permutation operator construction not independently verified in detail
Not independently reproduced; code logic is sound so no reason to doubt
Not tested; paper correctly identifies as open
